top10.introHtml: Ranked for the cheap fun second car lens: working top condition first, then reliable model family, manual preferred, mileage flexible. Diversity across sources and body styles.

picks.whyGood: Pontiac Solstice shares the Kappa platform with the Saturn Sky — lightweight, RWD, Ecotec 2.4L. At 63k miles and a Great Deal rating, this is near the top of the range for this car's reliability sweet spot. Excellent price for a fun weekend roadster with genuine collectible appeal.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through full open/close cycle; check for torn headliner and carpet water staining; inspect rear plastic window for yellowing/cracks; look at rocker panels for rust; check coolant for oil contamination (head gasket); confirm timing chain condition.
picks.bottomLine: Best-value fun car in this search — great price, low miles, reliable platform.

picks.whyGood: Remarkably low 37k miles on a 2008 Solstice just 9 miles away. Late-model Solstices had improved interior quality. Good Deal rating and extremely low mileage for the money make this a standout local find.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Kappa platform checks: top operation, rear window, rocker rust, coolant condition. With low miles, verify car was not stored improperly (dry-rotted seals, old fuel residue).
picks.bottomLine: 37k-mile Solstice 9 miles from home — that's a unicorn in this price range.

picks.whyGood: BMW 128i convertible with 69k miles at $9,500 is a remarkable value. The N52 inline-six is one of BMW's most reliable engines. Great Deal rating confirmed. 1-Series convertible is a true driver's car with reasonable running costs compared to M-badged siblings.
picks.whatToVerify: Check hydraulic soft-top rams for leaks; test all 4 windows; inspect cooling system (coolant flush history); check for oil leaks at valve cover and oil filter housing; confirm clutch/trans if manual; look for water intrusion in trunk.
picks.bottomLine: Rare great-value German roadster — reliable N52 six, low miles, strong deal rating.

picks.whyGood: 2010 BMW 128i convertible at $7,995 — entry price for a proper BMW convertible with the silky N52 six. 90k miles is mid-life for this engine. Good Deal rating. The E88 128i is the sweet spot of affordable BMW convertible ownership.
picks.whatToVerify: Top hydraulics (actuator rods under rear seat cushion), N52 coolant system (plastic water pump impeller), VANOS rattles on cold start, check for ICV codes, inspect soft top fabric for tears.
picks.bottomLine: Under $8k for a proper BMW convertible — hard to beat if the top hardware is solid.

picks.whyGood: GXP trim means the turbocharged LNF 2.0T — 260hp in a 2,900lb roadster. Great Deal rating at $8,590 with 88k miles. Final year of production adds some rarity. The LNF is more complex than the base Ecotec but tremendously fun.
picks.whatToVerify: Turbo GXP specifics: inspect intercooler for oil fouling, check boost levels (should hit ~14psi), look for boost creep issues, top condition checks same as base Solstice.
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged sleeper roadster at a great-deal price — one of the most fun per dollar in this search.

picks.whyGood: E46 325Ci convertible with just 60k miles — these are getting harder to find clean. Clean title + no accidents verified. The M54 inline-six is bulletproof at this mileage. E46 convertibles have a dedicated following and strong parts/community support.
picks.whatToVerify: E46 specifics: subframe reinforcement check (critical — look for cracks at rear subframe mounting points), cooling system (replace if not done: thermostat, water pump, expansion tank), soft top mechanism, check for oil pan gasket and rear main seal leaks.
picks.bottomLine: 60k-mile E46 325Ci with clean history — one of the best all-around driver's convertibles under $15k.

picks.whyGood: 32,350 miles on a 2006 Solstice — essentially a time capsule at $12,995. Clean title and no accidents on Cars.com. The Solstice at this mileage will feel brand new. Priced at a premium for condition but justified by the odometer.
picks.whatToVerify: Despite low miles, check for dried/cracked top seals (car may have sat), inspect tires for age (dry rot), verify all rubber components, confirm battery health, check that top motor has been exercised regularly.
picks.bottomLine: 32k-mile Solstice is like new — pay the premium for peace of mind.

picks.whyGood: 5th-gen Camaro 1LT convertible just 8 miles away, Good Deal rated. The 3.6L V6 makes 323hp — plenty of fun. 5th-gen Camaro convertibles are great-looking and spacious compared to Miata-sized roadsters. 95k is mid-life for this drivetrain.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate power top through full cycle; check rear window clarity; inspect for water intrusion at trunk seal; look at convertible top frame alignment (5th-gen tops are complex); check LFX V6 for oil consumption; listen for lifter tick.
picks.bottomLine: Local V6 Camaro convertible at a good price — proper American muscle drop-top under $12k.

picks.whyGood: 2012 BMW 128i convertible at 75k miles — later build year than the $7,995 example with lower mileage. Fair Deal rating (not overpriced). These E88s are aging gracefully and remain one of the best-handling affordable convertibles available.
picks.whatToVerify: Same E88 checks: hydraulic top condition, N52 cooling system, VANOS, check DSC/ABS modules, inspect roof drain tubes for clogging.
picks.bottomLine: Solid mid-tier BMW convertible option — good mileage for the year, right price.

picks.whyGood: 2007 GXP turbo with only 51,730 miles at $12,294. Second year of GXP production with any early-build quirks sorted. Low mileage for a turbocharged roadster — these are increasingly sought after as modern classic fun cars.
picks.whatToVerify: Turbo LNF checks: intercooler condition, boost pressure, top mechanism, rocker panels, any TPMS or ABS warning lights, transmission (manual preferred — check synchros).
picks.bottomLine: Low-mile GXP turbo within 20 miles — compelling if the boost system checks out.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in budget. GT/V8 trims ranked first; V6 models follow. SN-95 (1994–2004) and S197 (2005–2014) both well represented in this search.

picks.whyGood: 1994 SN-95 GT convertible with only 44,000 miles — remarkably preserved. 5.0L pushrod V8 predecessor in a classic body. Clean title, no accidents. SN-95 Mustangs are approaching classic status and this low-mileage example is special.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 frame rust at convertible top mount-to-rocker junction (critical), check T-45 transmission for third-gear synchro wear, look for rear seat water staining from top seal, inspect convertible top mounting bar condition, check 5.0/4.6 swap history.
picks.bottomLine: 44k-mile SN-95 GT is a legitimate collectible at a fair price.

picks.whyGood: 1995 Mustang GT at 55k miles from a Philadelphia dealer — same SN-95 platform, late first-gen GT with the 5.0L. Similar value to the '94 but slightly higher mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 checks: rocker/frame rust, top mount corrosion, T-45 gearbox synchros, rear axle clunk on takeoff (8.8 rear), check top for tears and rear window delamination.
picks.bottomLine: Solid SN-95 GT alternative if the '94 is gone — same great V8, similar value.

picks.whyGood: 2013 V6 Mustang convertible with 64k miles at $10,499 — the 305hp Cyclone V6 is actually more fuel-efficient than you'd expect and plenty quick. Clean title, no accidents. S197 Mustangs are bulletproof daily drivers.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible checks: IRS clunk if equipped (most S197 convertibles had solid rear axle), top hydraulic operation, check for subframe corrosion, 6R80 auto trans fluid condition, listen for any exhaust manifold tick.
picks.bottomLine: Modern, reliable V6 Mustang convertible at a fair price — great daily/weekend driver.
